name: Webview theme tokens
description: Shared design-token layer for all extension webviews. webviewTheme.ts exports themeTokenDecls(surface: 'sidebar' | 'editor') which emits the CSS custom-property block (--bg-*, --fg-*, --clay*, --border*, --ok/--warn/--danger, fonts, radii) derived from the active VS Code theme's --vscode-* variables via color-mix(), with the former Paper & Clay palette values as fallbacks. Consumed by reactWebview.ts (React sidebar views + editor pages, surface param), panelTheme.ts (string-built sidebar panels), prefsPanelHtml.ts, globalPrefsPanelHtml.ts, and brandView.ts. The Paper & Clay theme itself was extracted to github.com/tesenwein/paper-and-clay-theme.
guidelines: Never hardcode palette hex/rgba values in webview CSS — reference the tokens, or derive tints with color-mix(in srgb, var(--token) N%, transparent).
Accent (--clay) maps to var(--vscode-focusBorder); semantic status colors map to --vscode-charts-* / --vscode-errorForeground with the legacy hex as fallback.
Sidebar webviews use surface 'sidebar' (sideBar-background base); editor-tab pages use 'editor' (editor-background base).
Elevation steps (--bg-1..3) and fg fades (--fg-1..4) are color-mix ratios of --fg-0 into --bg-0 — they work in both light and dark themes; keep color-scheme: light dark.
anti_patterns: Adding a new webview host with its own hardcoded :root palette instead of importing themeTokenDecls.
Using rgba(217,119,87, …) or other Paper & Clay literals in panel CSS.
